title: Temple of Vedic Planetarium
tagline: Temple of Vedic Planetarium — spiritual town of West Bengal

heroImage.alt: Temple of Vedic Planetarium, West Bengal

overview.short: The Temple of the Vedic Planetarium is a modern Hindu temple and planetarium complex under construction since 2009 in Mayapur, West Bengal, India.
overview.description: The Temple of the Vedic Planetarium is a modern Hindu temple and planetarium complex under construction since 2009 in Mayapur, West Bengal, India. Serving as the global headquarters of the International Society for Krishna Consciousness (ISKCON), the temple is of particular significance to followers of Gaudiya Vaishnavism, a tradition associated with Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. The complex includes the Sri Mayapur Chandrodaya Mandir, one of the main temples within the premises, which is dedicated to the worship of the Pancha Tattva deities, Radha Madhava along with the Ashta-sakhis, and Narasimha. U

topPlaces.name: Maheshganj
topPlaces.description: Maheshganj is a village in the Nabadwip CD block in the Krishnanagar Sadar subdivision of the Nadia district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Mayapur
topPlaces.description: Mayapur (Mayāpura) is a pilgrimage town in the Nabadwip CD block in the Krishnanagar Sadar subdivision of the Nadia district, West Bengal, India. It is situated at the confluence of the Jalangi River and the Bhagirathi, a distributary of the Ganges.

topPlaces.name: Jalangi River
topPlaces.description: Jalangi River (Bengali: জলঙ্গী নদী) is a branch of the Ganges river in Murshidabad and Nadia districts in the Indian state of West Bengal. It flows into the Bhagirathi river and strengthens its lower channel, the Hooghly.

topPlaces.name: Nabadwip
topPlaces.description: Nabadwip (Bengali pronunciation: [nɔbodːip]), also spelt Navadwip, historically known as Nadia, is a heritage city in Nadia district in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is regarded as a holy place by Hindus, and is the birthplace of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu.

topPlaces.name: Char Maijdia
topPlaces.description: Char Maijdia is a census town in the Nabadwip CD block in the Krishnanagar Sadar subdivision of the Nadia district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Gadigachha
topPlaces.description: Gadigachha (also referred to as Gadigachhi) is a census town in the Nabadwip CD block in the Krishnanagar Sadar subdivision of the Nadia district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Tiorkhali
topPlaces.description: Tiorkhali is a census town in the Nabadwip CD block in the Krishnanagar Sadar subdivision of the Nadia district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Majdia, Nabadwip
topPlaces.description: Majdia is a census town in the Nabadwip CD block in the Krishnanagar Sadar subdivision of the Nadia district in the state of West Bengal, India.

items.note: Maheshganj is a village in the Nabadwip CD block in the Krishnanagar Sadar subdivision of the Nadia district in the state of West Bengal, India.

Mayapur (Mayāpura) is a pilgrimage town in the Nabadwip CD block in the Krishnanagar Sadar subdivision of the Nadia district, West Bengal, India. It is situated at the confluence of the Jalangi River and the Bhagirathi, a distributary of the Ganges.

Jalangi River (Bengali: জলঙ্গী নদী) is a branch of the Ganges river in Murshidabad and Nadia districts in the Indian state of West Bengal. It flows into the Bhagirathi river and strengthens its lower channel, the Hooghly.

Nabadwip (Bengali pronunciation: [nɔbodːip]), also spelt Navadwip, historically known as Nadia, is a heritage city in Nadia district in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is regarded as a holy place by Hindus, and is the birthplace of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Temple of Vedic Planetarium?
faq.a: The best time to visit Temple of Vedic Planetarium is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Temple of Vedic Planetarium?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (86 km) and the nearest railway station is Howrah Junction (Kolkata) (94 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Temple of Vedic Planetarium?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Temple of Vedic Planetarium at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Temple of Vedic Planetarium?
faq.a: Stays in Temple of Vedic Planetarium start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Temple of Vedic Planetarium?
faq.a: The top attractions are Maheshganj, Mayapur, Jalangi River, Nabadwip, Char Maijdia.
